data.title: Boeing begins formal certification trials of engine anti-ice fix for 737 aircraft, Air Current says
data.description:
Boeing has begun formal certification trials of the newly revised engine anti-ice design fix on its lead 737 Max 10 test aircraft, the Air Current reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the program.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report.

data.title: Philippines Consumption Likely Weighed by Higher Oil Prices — Market Talk
data.description: Higher oil prices will likely weigh on the Philippines' domestic consumption and household purchasing power, analysts at BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions, say in a note. Headline inflation jumped to 4.1% in March, breaching Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas' 4% ceiling, due to rising domestic diesel prices. Diesel prices have risen by 80% compared with preconflict levels, BMI notes. "Spending will remain influenced by the elevated inflationary pressures as well as currently high debt levels, along with related debt servicing costs," BMI says. It forecasts the Philippines' real household spending growth to slow to 4.5% in 2026, down from 2025's 4.7%. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: CB: Earnings and book value soared on strong underwriting and investment results, despite market headwinds
data.description:
Net income and core operating income surged over 74% and 80% year-over-year, with strong premium growth and improved underwriting margins. Book value and tangible book value per share rose sharply, while management highlighted market softening and global risks.

data.title: Johnson Electric Profit Alert Prompts Deutsche to Cut Target Price — Market Talk
data.description: Johnson Electric's profit alert for its FY ended March came in below expectations due to higher costs and non-cash charges, Deutsche Bank analyst Bin Wang says in a note. The company indicates it will continue implementing cost-reduction measures such as automation and product standardization to offset headwinds from higher raw material costs, Wang says. However, it remains well positioned in the humanoid robotics market, with early-stage sampling underway with multiple customers, including start-ups and automakers in China and overseas. Deutsche Bank cuts its target price to HK$46.0 from HK$49.0 and maintains a buy call on the stock, which is down 14.25% at HK$22.02. (venkat.pr@wsj.com)

data.title: Japan's Goods Trade Faces Destabilizing Headwinds — Market Talk
data.description: Japan's goods trade has hovered close to balance for the past six months, but that's unlikely to last, says Stefan Angrick at Moody's Analytics. A weak yen and the artificial-intelligence boom buoyed exports, while subdued domestic demand kept a lid on imports. But the Middle East war has pushed up commodity prices, inflating Japan's import bill. If hostilities end soon, the inflation impact and economic damage could be limited, but uncertainty is high. U.S. tariffs and trade threats from China are added concerns. At home, wage growth is weak, and an inflation surge could limit pay gains and cap demand. Moody's Analytics expects exports to slow as foreign demand softens and commodity prices raise production costs, while import values are tipped to rise as Japan's import bill swells. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

data.title: Cathay Pacific plans Hong Kong dollar three-year bond, term sheet shows
data.description:
Hong Kong's Cathay Pacific plans to issue benchmark-sized three-year Hong Kong dollar senior unsecured fixed-rate notes, showed a term sheet reviewed by Reuters on Wednesday, with initial price guidance set in the 4.1% area.

The bonds will mature on April 29, 2029, pay interest semi-annually and are due to settle on April 29, 2026, the sheet showed.

Proceeds will be used for on-lending to the airline and subsidiaries for working capital and general corporate purposes, the sheet showed.

Bank of China (Hong Kong) (BOCHK.UL), BNP Paribas , DBS and HSBC are joint bookrunners and joint lead managers.

Cathay Pacific did not respond to an emailed request for comment.

data.title: This week at SpaceX: AI bets, losses and push for control as mega IPO looms
data.description:
Elon Musk's SpaceX saw a slew of developments this week as it barrels toward what could be the largest initial public offering in history.

The rocket and satellite company disclosed an option to acquire AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion, outlined plans that would entrench permanent voting control for founder Musk, and is kicking off a three-day Wall Street analyst roadshow aimed at defending a $1.75 trillion valuation.

SpaceX is targeting a late-June market IPO with a $75 billion capital raising, even as excerpts from its confidential IPO filing show the company swung to a multi-billion-dollar loss in 2025, driven by heavy spending on artificial intelligence.

Here are some major developments:

  • Cursor deal: SpaceX announced on Tuesday it has been granted the option to either acquire code-generation startup Cursor for $60 billion later this year, or pay $10 billion for a partnership.

  • Voting control: Reuters reported on Monday that SpaceX plans to hand Musk and some insiders super-voting shares that will outweigh other investors after the IPO.

  • Financials: SpaceX swung to a $4.94 billion consolidated loss in 2025 on revenue of $18.67 billion. SpaceX and xAI, which merged earlier this year, ended 2025 with about $24.8 billion in cash on hand, assets of $92 billion, and liabilities of $50.8 billion. Capex increased almost five-fold over two years to $20.74 billion.

  • Analyst roadshows: SpaceX is kicking off a three-day Wall Street analyst roadshow this week to justify the $1.75 trillion valuation it is seeking in its IPO.

  • Warnings to investors: SpaceX has warned investors that its ambitions to build space-based AI data centers, as well as human settlements on the moon and Mars, rely on unproven technologies and may not become commercially viable, Reuters reported on Tuesday.

  • Musk, executives' pay: Musk was paid $54,080 last year but stands to gain billions in equity after the IPO. The Information reported that Musk bought $1.4 billion of stock from employees last year. Reuters reported that SpaceX President Gwynne Shotwell earned $85.8 million in total compensation last year, placing her among the highest-paid U.S. executives. CFO Bret Johnsen earned $9.8 million.

  • Index rejigs: The mega IPO has also driven index providers to consider revising their approach to designing market indexes, with Morningstar Inc saying its CRSP Market Indexes will add an "alternative liquidity screen", making it possible to add SpaceX and other giant IPOs to the benchmarks more rapidly.

  • Retail push: Musk plans to allocate about 30% of shares to retail investors. Around 1,500 retail investors have been invited to tour its Starbase launch facilities in Texas, after the roadshow begins in the week of June 8. International retail access will be extended to the UK, the European Union, Australia, Canada, Japan, and South Korea.

data.title: Malaysia's KK Mart Retail Files IPO Prospectus
data.description:
(01:25 GMT) *Malaysia's KK Mart Retail Files IPO Draft Prospectus
(01:26 GMT) *KK Mart to Use IPO Proceeds for Expansion and Digital Push
(01:44 GMT) Malaysia's KK Mart Retail Files IPO Prospectus

By Ying Xian Wong

KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia--One of Malaysia's largest convenience-store operators, KK Mart Retail, has submitted a draft prospectus for an initial public offering, joining a steady pipeline of companies seeking to tap investor demand despite the Mideast conflict.

The company plans to offer up to 840 million shares, comprising 630 million existing shares and 210 million new shares, according to the prospectus dated April 20. The IPO will include an institutional offering of up to 735 million shares and a retail portion of 105 million shares.

Dow Jones Newswires had earlier reported on the company's listing plans.

The draft prospectus didn't disclose the financial terms of the offer, but people familiar with the process said earlier that the offering would give the company a market value of around $750 million.

The Southeast Asian country hosted 60 listings in 2025, the most in two decades, and 20 companies have listed so far this year.

KK Mart operates "KK Super Mart", a modernized mom-and-pop store offering daily essentials 24 hours a day, with nearly 1,000 outlets across the country. KK Super Mart also has outlets in India and Nepal.

The company reported a net profit of 96.99 million ringgit, equivalent to $24.5 million, for the financial year ended June 30, 2025.

Proceeds from the public issue would be used to expand new stores, invest in distribution center capacity, strengthen digital and IT capabilities, repay borrowings, and cover listing expenses, the prospectus said.

Maybank Investment Bank is the principal adviser for the IPO.

Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com

data.title: Gold rises as oil weakens after US extends ceasefire with Iran
data.description:
Gold prices rose on Wednesday after hitting a one-week low in the previous session, helped by lower oil prices after the U.S. extended a ceasefire with Iran, easing fears of an inflation spike and higher-for-longer interest rates.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Spot gold rose 0.6% to $4,739.94 per ounce, as of 0105 GMT, after falling to its lowest level since April 13 in the previous session.

* U.S. gold futures for June delivery gained 0.8% to $4,758.60.

* U.S. President Donald Trump said on Tuesday he would indefinitely extend the ceasefire with Iran, hours before it was set to expire. Trump's announcement appeared to be unilateral, and it was not immediately clear whether Iran or the U.S. ally Israel would agree to extend the ceasefire, which began two weeks ago.

* Stocks gained, dollar eased and oil prices turned lower following the ceasefire extension.

* Federal Reserve chief nominee Kevin Warsh said on Tuesday he had made no promises to Trump about cutting interest rates, as he tried to assure U.S. senators mulling his confirmation to lead the central bank that he would act independently of the White House while pursuing broad reforms.

* Meanwhile, Trump on Tuesday told CNBC that he would be disappointed if Kevin Warsh did not cut interest rates right away once he took office after being approved by the Senate.

* Gold exports from Switzerland rose 30% month-on-month in March, as shipments to Britain jumped to their highest since December and supplies to China, a major bullion consumer, increased by 18%.

* U.S. retail sales increased more than expected in March as the war with Iran boosted gasoline prices and receipts at service stations, while tax refunds supported spending elsewhere.

* SPDR Gold Trust , the world's largest gold-backed exchange-traded fund, said its holdings fell 0.1% to 965.94 metric tons on Monday.

* Spot silver rose 1% to $77.47 per ounce, platinum gained 0.8% to $2,052.99, and palladium was up 1.3% at $1,552.75.

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

EU Consumer Confid. Flash Apr

data.title: First Tick: Top global cues to watch in today’s trade
data.description:
Indian equity benchmarks, the BSE Sensex and Nifty 50, are likely to open on a weak note on April 22, tracking positive cues from GIFT Nifty, which is trading higher at around 24,400.

Track the latest updates on GIFT Nifty right here on Moneycontrol.

Indian benchmark indices ended higher for the third consecutive session on April 21, supported by broad-based buying across sectors, as reports of a second round of talks between the U.S. and Iran boosted hopes of a resolution to the ongoing conflict.

At close, the Sensex was up 753.03 points or 0.96 percent at 79,273.33, and the Nifty was up 211.75 points or 0.87 percent at 24,576.60.

Here is how financial markets across the globe fared overnight:

GIFT Nifty (Down)

GIFT Nifty was trading lower at around 24,400, indicating a negative start for the Indian equity markets.

Asian Equities (Down)

Stocks in Asia edged lower at the open, as investors grappled with how long the Middle East conflict may continue. The MSCI Asia Pacific Index slipped 0.3%.

US Equities (Slip)

US stocks closed lower on Tuesday, with early gains evaporating as renewed concerns about ​the Middle East war outweighed initial optimism over a round of solid corporate earnings.

Iran could attend talks with the United States in Pakistan if ‌Washington abandons its policy of pressure and threats, a senior Iranian official told Reuters, adding that Tehran rejects negotiations aimed at surrender.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 293.18 points, or 0.59%, to 49,149.38, the S&P 500 lost 45.13 points, or 0.63%, to 7,064.01 and the Nasdaq Composite ​lost 144.43 points, or 0.59%, to 24,259.96.

Dollar Index (Up)

The dollar steadied in early Asian trade on Wednesday, as skepticism over U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement of an indefinite extension of a ceasefire with Iran lifted demand for the safe-haven currency, pushing it to a one-week high.

US Bond Yield (Down)

The yield on 10-year Treasuries were down marginally at 4.28%, while yield on 2-year Treasuries were down 1 basis point at 3.76%.

Asian Currencies (Mixed)

Asian currencies were trading mixed in the early trade on Wednesday with Malaysian Ringgit, Taiwan Dollar, Philippines Peso, Thai Baht, China Renminbi were trading lower, while Indonesian Rupiah, Singapore Dollar, South Korean Won were trading with gains.

Crude (Down)

Brent Crude and US West Texas crude prices were down marginally in the early trade on Wednesday.

Gold (Up)

Gold held a two-day decline after US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran and plans for a fresh round of peace talks fell apart.

Fund Flow Action

Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) extended their selling for the second consecutive session on April 21, offloading equities worth nearly ₹2,000 crore. In contrast, Domestic Institutional Investors (DIIs) provided support to the market, purchasing equities worth over ₹2,000 crore.

Hope you're all set for today's trade. We wish you a profitable day ahead.

Disclaimer

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Chicago soy rises as oil prices climb, rain forecast threatens Midwest planting
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade soybean futures gained on Wednesday on higher oil prices and forecasts of rain later this week in the U.S. Midwest that could slow planting, while corn also climbed and wheat dropped.

FUNDAMENTALS

* The most-active soybeans contract on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) was up 0.3% at $11.94-1/4 a bushel, as of 0120 GMT. Wheat edged 0.1% lower to $6.12-1/4 a bushel, and corn added 0.2% to $4.63 a bushel.

* Weather concerns have supported soybean and corn prices.

* Dry weather is expected in the central U.S. over the coming days, but rain is forecast to return late this week, likely stalling spring field work, meteorologists said.

* Rising crude oil prices have also underpinned grain markets.

* U.S. crude futures rose in early trade on Wednesday, despite Washington's announcement that it would indefinitely extend a ceasefire with Iran, as uncertainty over peace talks persisted and the Strait of Hormuz remained effectively closed.

* In Ukraine, a key grains exporting region, a cold snap could slow or halt the sowing of late spring crops including corn, the trading department of Ukraine's largest farmers' union UAC said on Tuesday.

* The U.S. Department of Agriculture confirmed private sales of 100,000 metric tons of U.S. corn to Colombia and 195,000 tons to undisclosed buyers for shipment in the 2025/26 marketing year.

* Commodity funds were net buyers of CBOT corn, wheat and soy, traders said on Tuesday.

MARKET NEWS

* U.S. stock futures rose and the dollar wavered after U.S. President Donald Trump said he would indefinitely extend the Iran ceasefire keeping sentiment buoyed although with the Strait of Hormuz still closed oil held onto its recent gains.

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

0600 UK Core CPI YY March

0600 UK CPI YY March

0600 UK CPI Services MM, YY March

1400 EU Consumer Confid. Flash April

data.title: Treasury Wine Still Faces Potential Distributor Disruption — Market Talk
data.description: Treasury Wine Estates still faces risk from potential disruption to U.S. distribution, RBC analyst Michael Toner warns. He sees the Australian producer's update on depletions, operational reorganization and debt refinancing as incrementally positive, but remains cautious over the possibility of further U.S. distribution problems. Toner tells clients in a note that demand is constrained globally and that key distributors are experiencing financial pressure. Treasury Wine previously announced it had repurchased inventory from a prior distributor. RBC has a last-published sector-perform rating and A$5.70 target price on the stock, which is up 15% at A$4.645. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: BHP Production Result Good, But Not Clean — Market Talk
data.description: BHP's 3Q result is "better than it looks, but not clean," RBC Capital Markets analyst Kaan Peker says in a note. "Iron ore did the heavy lifting," he says. Peker highlights better-than-expected realized prices across commodities, which he says help mask a mixed operational outcome. In copper, Antamina is the standout, outperforming on both grades and throughput, says Peker. "The issue is Spence," he says. Recoveries and production there are worse than he expects. Peker adds that the "balance sheet is the quiet positive," while proceeds from the Wheaton streaming deal should support capital returns. RBC has a sector perform rating and A$56/share target on BHP. Shares are up 0.7% at A$55.90. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)
